title: malva × turolensis
intro: Malva × turolensis, also known as the mallow hybrid, is a plant species derived from the cross between Malva sylvestris and Malva flexuosa. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help soothe skin irritations, wounds, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Malva × turolensis include its ability to reduce swelling, promote wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders such as diarrhea and irritable bowel syndrome. This plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and topical creams, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
